$description: SRF (Self-Realization Fellowship) visual language. Two complementary voices serve SRF's dual mission of meditation and community. The Contemplative Voice (gold, navy, cream) is the library — deep reading, spiritual stillness, inner illumination. Derived from yogananda.org and the Online Meditation Center. The Communal Voice (marigold, charcoal, white) is the gathering — convocations, events, joyful welcome. Derived from convocation.yogananda.org. Both voices share the same semantic rules (calm technology, emotional registers, accessibility); each composes different accent, text, and background palettes. The contemplative voice is the default for reading surfaces; the communal voice serves event and outreach surfaces.

color.$description: SRF color palette. Two accent pillars: gold (contemplative — the halo, inner light) and marigold (communal — the garland, warm welcome). Navy and charcoal serve as text colors for each voice. Cream backgrounds for contemplation; white for gathering.

gold.$description: SRF Gold. The halo, the third eye's light, spiritual warmth. Primary accent across all themes. Usage: focus rings, active links, decorative marks, ornamental elements.

navy.$description: SRF Navy. The depth of meditation, the night sky of inner stillness. Primary text color in light themes. Background in dark themes.

cream.$description: Warm Cream. Never pure white — always warm. Digital paper, a library's ambient light. Primary background in light theme.

cream-dark.$description: Secondary cream. Cards, sidebars, secondary surfaces. Subtle differentiation without hard boundaries.

surface.$description: Surface white. Used sparingly — elevated cards in light theme. Even this is warmed by the cream context.

marigold.$description: SRF Marigold. The warmth of community gathering, the burnt orange of marigold garlands offered in devotion. Primary accent for the communal voice — event surfaces, convocation pages, community-facing UX. Where gold says 'contemplate,' marigold says 'welcome.' Passes WCAG AA for large text on white (3.4:1); use for buttons, headings, and interactive elements, not body text.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Extracted from convocation.yogananda.org (2026). SRF uses this orange for all event and community surfaces. Marigold connects to Indian devotional tradition — garlands offered at temples, the warmth of communal worship.
org.yogananda.design.evaluationTrigger: If SRF event sites shift away from this orange, re-evaluate.

marigold-hover.$description: Marigold deepened for hover and active states. The pressed-petal intensity of engagement. Passes WCAG AA for large text on white (4.2:1).

marigold-light.$description: Warm peach. The communal voice's secondary surface — like cream but orange-warm rather than yellow-warm. Event page section backgrounds, highlighted content on gathering surfaces.

charcoal.$description: Warm charcoal. Neutral body text for the communal voice — approachable, readable, emotionally unloaded. Where navy carries the depth of meditation, charcoal carries no metaphor; it simply serves readability. WCAG AAA on white (8.5:1).

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Extracted from convocation.yogananda.org body text. SRF uses this neutral gray for community surfaces where navy's contemplative weight would feel overly formal.

charcoal-secondary.$description: Charcoal at 64% opacity. Secondary text in the communal voice. Parallels navy text-secondary in the contemplative voice.

gold-dark.$description: Amber gold. A bridge between gold and marigold — gold deepened toward warmth. Appropriate for large text and decorative elements on white backgrounds (3.0:1). Not for body text — use charcoal or navy for that.

border-neutral.$description: Neutral gray border for the communal voice. Where contemplative borders derive from navy at 12% opacity (blue-tinted), these are true neutral — clean, modern, uncolored.

gold-opacity.$description: Gold at calibrated attention levels. A deliberate hierarchy from interactive (demands attention) to subliminal (felt, not seen). This gradient expresses how gold serves different roles without changing its hue.

interactive.$description: Focus rings, active links, call-to-action accents. Full presence — the seeker is engaging.

decorative.$description: Epigraph quotation marks, chapter ornaments. Present but not calling — background beauty.

ambient.$description: Scroll progress indicator, meditate-theme gold. Peripheral awareness — orientation without distraction.

highlight.$description: Keyboard-navigated paragraph outline. Guiding the reader, not grabbing them.

subliminal.$description: Paragraph hover background, dwell mode highlight. Barely visible — registers as warmth, not color.

texture.$description: Paper texture noise overlay (SVG fractal noise). The ghost of physical pages.

marigold-opacity.$description: Marigold at calibrated community-engagement levels. Parallels gold-opacity but for the communal voice. Where gold dissolves into contemplative atmosphere, marigold dissolves into welcoming warmth.

interactive.$description: CTAs, registration buttons, primary actions. Full warmth — the community is inviting you in.

decorative.$description: Section dividers, icon tints, schedule markers. Present but not urgent — the event continues around you.

ambient.$description: Background accent tints, card hover states (bg-accent/20). The warmth of a room full of fellow seekers.

subliminal.$description: Subtle background warmth on white surfaces. The faintest glow of shared purpose.

category.$description: Categorical color set for multi-track content: event schedules, program types, content classifications. Four distinct hues that complement the marigold accent without competing. Extracted from the Convocation 2026 schedule interface.

teal.$description: Teal. Cool, grounded — meditation sessions, quiet events, contemplative programs. The still water complement to marigold's warm fire.

indigo.$description: Soft indigo. Engaged learning — classes, workshops, study circles. The blue of active intellect in service of understanding.

amber.$description: Dark amber. A deeper, earthier gold — keynote events, featured talks, distinguished content. Bridges gold's spiritual gravitas with the communal context.

theme.$description: Seven complete color themes. Six for the contemplative voice (light, sepia, earth, dark, meditate) plus one for the communal voice (gathering). Each has an emotional purpose. data-theme attribute on <html> selects the active theme. Auto follows OS preference.

light.$description: Default. A warm library in daylight. Contemplative cool with scholarly warmth.

text-secondary.$description: Navy at 70% opacity. color-mix(in srgb, #1a2744 70%, transparent).

sepia.$description: Antique paper. The feeling of holding a physical book — yellowed pages, warm ink. For readers who want to feel the weight of the volume.

earth.$description: YSS-inspired proof-of-concept (PRO-043). Warm clay and terracotta accent. Indian warmth — the red earth of Ranchi, not the Pacific blue of Encinitas.

gold.$description: In earth theme, the accent is YSS terracotta, not SRF gold.

dark.$description: Deep navy. Respectful of the brand in darkness — not generic dark mode, but SRF navy deepened. For evening reading and reduced eye strain.

gold.$description: Gold brightened for dark backgrounds. Maintains warmth without muddiness.

meditate.$description: Deeper than dark. For contemplation (DES-011). The deepest blue of pre-dawn meditation. Gold subdued to 60% — even the accent becomes quiet.

gold.$description: Gold at 60% opacity. Even the accent retreats in meditation.

gathering.$description: The communal voice. Event sites, convocation pages, community-facing surfaces. Marigold accent on clean white — open, warm, modern, welcoming. Where the light theme is a library in daylight, the gathering theme is the temple courtyard alive with fellow seekers. Not a reading theme but a doing theme — registering, scheduling, connecting, arriving.

bg.$description: Pure white. The communal voice embraces clean white — open, modern, unintimidating. Not the library's aged paper but the courtyard's clear light.

bg-secondary.$description: Warm peach (marigold-light). Sections differentiated by a breath of orange warmth.

border.$description: Neutral gray borders. Clean lines without navy's contemplative tint.

gold.$description: In gathering theme, the accent slot is SRF marigold. The communal voice speaks in orange, not gold.

surface.$description: White surfaces. Cards and elevated elements share the open white ground.

circadian.$description: Time-aware background shifts (DES-011). Subtle warmth changes based on time of day. Only active in light theme. The portal breathes with the seeker's day.

morning.$description: 5:00–9:59. Cooler cream — the clarity of early light. Blue channel rises, red recedes. The cool stillness before the day warms.

midday.$description: 10:00–15:59. Default palette — no override needed. Full natural light.

evening.$description: 16:00–20:59. Warmer cream — golden hour warmth. Amber rises, blue recedes. The day exhaling toward rest.

night.$description: 21:00–4:59. Circadian attribute removed; dark theme applies if auto-theme is active.

typography.$description: SRF type system. Four roles: reading (immersive body text), display (titles and headers), display-event (communal voice headings), UI (navigation and chrome). The contemplative voice uses Merriweather/Lora for reading and display; the communal voice uses ArcherPro for display and Helvetica Neue for UI. Both voices share the reading stack for any sacred or reverential text.

reading.$description: Primary reading font. Merriweather for its warm serifs and excellent screen rendering at small sizes. 1.8 line height for comfortable sustained reading. The seeker may read for hours — this must never tire the eyes.

reading-light.$description: Light reading weight. Epigraphs, block quotes, citations. A softer voice — the text recedes slightly, signaling 'supporting context' rather than 'primary teaching.'

display.$description: Chapter titles, section headers. Lora for its calligraphic warmth — more expressive than Merriweather at large sizes. Bold to anchor the page.

ui.$description: Navigation, labels, system text. Open Sans for clarity and neutrality — it disappears. The UI typography should never compete with the reading typography for attention.

ui-semibold.$description: Button labels, active nav items, emphasis in UI context. Semibold, not bold — assertive without shouting.

citation.$description: Attribution lines (PRI-02). Author, book, chapter, page. Small, light, always present. Preceded by em-dash + non-breaking space (U+2014 U+00A0).

display-inspirational.$description: Devotional display font. Asar — ancient Indian stone-inscription aesthetic. Chisel on stone, temple walls, Ashoka's edicts. The font that says 'these words endure.' Covers both Latin and Devanagari in a single devotional voice. Self-hosted under OFL. Falls back to Merienda for calligraphic warmth. Two metaphors of the sacred: the inscription and the handwritten letter.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Asar provides cultural authenticity — its stone-inscription aesthetic is rooted in Indian tradition and covers both Latin and Devanagari. Merienda is the fallback for calligraphic warmth. The two-font cascade degrades gracefully: ancient inscription → handcrafted warmth.
org.yogananda.design.evaluationTrigger: If SRF develops a custom devotional typeface, re-evaluate.

display-event.$description: Communal voice display font. ArcherPro — a transitional serif with humanist warmth and subtle slab-serif geometry from Hoefler&Co. More personality than Lora at large sizes; more approachability than Merriweather. SRF uses this for convocation headings and event marketing. The font that says 'you are invited.' Falls back to Georgia on systems without the licensed font.

org.yogananda.design.rationale: Extracted from convocation.yogananda.org (2026). ArcherPro is licensed by SRF from Hoefler&Co. Not all consumers will have access — Georgia is the closest freely available fallback with similar transitional warmth.
org.yogananda.design.evaluationTrigger: If SRF changes event site typography or if the license lapses, re-evaluate.

ui-event.$description: Communal voice UI font. Helvetica Neue at the distinctive 375 weight (between light and regular) that the Convocation site uses. Cleaner and more neutral than Open Sans — appropriate for event interfaces where the content is schedules, forms, and logistics rather than sacred text. The slight lightness signals 'infrastructure' without feeling thin.

font-size-scale.$description: Reader font size preferences. Users can scale up from the 16px base. Three levels: default (16px base), large (18px / 112.5%), larger (20px / 125%).

large.$description: 18px effective base. For seekers who prefer larger text.

larger.$description: 20px effective base. Maximum enlargement before layout stress.

line-spacing-scale.$description: Reader line spacing preferences. Applied to .reader-content. Three levels matching reading comfort needs.

favicon.$description: Gold lotus on navy. Canonical files: motifs/srf/lotus.svg (inline SVG), brand/apple-icon-spec.json (180x180 PNG generation spec). The lotus is the unified visual motif (DES-016) — it appears as favicon, section dividers, loading states, and ornamental accents.

high-contrast.$description: prefers-contrast: more overrides. Pure black/white text for maximum readability. Replaces all opacity-based text colors with solid values.
